title: barringtonia globosa
intro: Barringtonia globosa, also known as the Putat or Sea Poison, is a large evergreen tree native to the tropical regions of Asia, particularly in the coastal areas of India, Malaysia, and the Philippines. The bark, leaves, and roots of this plant possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral activities. The health benefits of Barringtonia globosa include its ability to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and fight against infections. The plant is often used in traditional medicine, and its herbal preparations include teas, decoctions, and infusions made from the bark, leaves, and roots, which are commonly consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
